calendarNotes:
Winston-Salem/Forsyth County Schools begins the 2026-2027 school year on August 10, 2026 — two weeks earlier than Charlotte-Mecklenburg and Guilford County, which start August 24–25. Spring Break runs March 26–April 2, 2027 — a full two weeks later than Wake County's March 15–19 break. The school year ends May 21, 2027, which is earlier than most North Carolina peers (Guilford County ends June 10, CMS ends June 9). Winston-Salem's earlier last day reflects its early August start combined with a slightly lower total instructional day count.

One of the most common points of confusion for families new to Winston-Salem is the relationship between WSFCS and Wake Forest University. Despite sharing 'Wake' in the name, Wake Forest University is a private research university physically located in Winston-Salem — not affiliated with Wake County or Wake County Public School System. Winston-Salem/Forsyth County Schools and Wake Forest University are entirely separate institutions. Wake Forest's semester calendar has no effect on WSFCS school dates.

Families coordinating Spring Break schedules between WSFCS and neighboring Guilford County Schools (Greensboro) should note the stagger: Guilford County Schools breaks March 22–26, while Winston-Salem doesn't start its break until March 26 (Good Friday) — meaning the two districts overlap for only the Good Friday holiday before both are in full spring break. Guilford County returns to school March 29, while Winston-Salem is still on break through April 2. This stagger matters for families with children in both districts.

Winston-Salem has historically been associated with R.J. Reynolds Tobacco and Krispy Kreme Doughnuts — both headquartered there — as well as a significant arts and healthcare sector driven by Wake Forest Baptist Medical Center. The district serves a diverse city whose population includes long-established African American communities and a growing Latino population. Families moving to Winston-Salem from other parts of NC or the country will find a city with a distinctive local identity separate from both Charlotte and the Research Triangle.
